reasoning: The community prediction was 3% in early April, and new information reinforces this low probability. Jerome Powell recently stated on March 18, 2026, that he intends to finish his term (which ends May 15, 2026) and will even stay on as 'chair pro tem' if his successor, Kevin Warsh, is not confirmed by then. Warsh's confirmation hearing was recently delayed (April 10), making an early confirmation unlikely. Based on historical precedent, even when a successor is confirmed early, outgoing Fed Chairs finish their exact terms (as Bernanke and Yellen did). Therefore, the only circumstances under which Powell ceases to be Chair before May 15 involve sudden mortality, a severe health emergency, or an extreme and unprecedented legal/executive removal by Trump that takes effect immediately without judicial stay. The baseline mortality/health risk over a 1-month period for a 73-year-old is roughly 0.2-0.5%, and the political risk is diminished by the proximity to the end of his term. I am submitting three identical probabilities since the resolution date parsing appears to reflect 'N/A' split into characters, and all refer to the same strict May 15, 2026 deadline.
